insight: Prohibition + form enumeration > prohibition alone. Anthropic enumerates all evasion forms (prose/summary/structured output fabrication). SuperClaude R15 says cite evidence but does not enumerate HOW agents evade.

context: CC source: do not fabricate in prose, summary, OR structured output — each form explicitly listed

insight: Self-review catches design-vs-implementation drift: missing R15 examples row and anti_misunderstanding wording inconsistency. When design defines rule text + examples table changes in same section, secondary item is easy to miss.

insight: Full brainstorm-to-commit pipeline validated: brainstorm → research → discovery → self-review (corrections) → design → implement → self-review (catch drift) → commit. 2 reviews caught 5 critical + 2 important issues before commit.
author: ajitta
context: ~280K tokens across main + 4 sub-agents. Pipeline prevents factual errors from reaching commit.

insight: W1 Phase 1: 5 agents gotchas added (self-review, system-architect, refactoring-expert, project-manager, root-cause-analyst). Gotchas count 5→10. 720/721 tests pass (1 pre-existing). 7 files changed.
author: ajitta

insight: R06 Scope chosen as secondary gotcha for 3/5 agents (self-review, system-architect, refactoring-expert). Scope creep is the most common cross-cutting agent failure mode, even though R06 was not in the design spec critical rule list (R02, R13, R15, R18).
author: ajitta
context: Design spec template mapped each agent category to one primary rule. R06 added as secondary based on observed agent behavior patterns.

insight: simplicity-guide.md places gotchas before handoff, deviating from agent-authoring.md convention (after handoff, before bounds). backend-architect follows convention correctly. Not corrected to avoid scope creep.
author: ajitta
context: agent-authoring.md says placement: after handoff, before bounds. simplicity-guide has gotchas→handoff→bounds order.

insight: Content-only framework changes (markdown gotchas) have near-zero test risk. All 720 structural tests pass without modification. Plan-to-implementation for content changes takes minutes vs hours for code changes.

ajitta

author: ajitta
insight: Phase 2 selective (5/13) over full rollout. Measurement gate impractical (no A/B for compaction drift), token cost trivial (~3 lines/agent), but educators+executors are low-judgment — gotchas have diminishing returns on procedural agents.
context: Original plan said measure Phase 1 first. Pragmatic decision: skip impractical gate, apply to high-value subset only.

author: ajitta
insight: R02 wording must be domain-differentiated even when the rule is identical: "Before implementing" (python) vs "Before configuring" (devops) vs "Before optimizing" (perf). Boilerplate R02 caught by self-review agent in iteration 2.
action: Changed devops-architect R02 from "Before implementing" to "Before configuring...infrastructure isn't already provisioned"

author: ajitta
insight: Effort/model alignment rule: Opus agents should have effort >= high. Sonnet agents should have effort <= medium. Mismatch signals configuration error
context: Derived from reviewing all 23 agents. Opus is chosen for judgment tasks that need deep reasoning — constraining effort contradicts the routing decision.

author: ajitta
insight: Leaked CC source TypeScript type shows 1-5|EffortValue union for effort field, but runtime YAML parser only accepts strings (low/medium/high/max). Type definition != runtime behavior
context: User asked to verify claim from leaked source. Web research + project history (cc-source-alignment-discovery spec SUPERSEDED note) confirm strings only at runtime.

author: ajitta
